metadata.name: Click-Through Booster
metadata.description: Audits all CTAs on the page (copy, placement, design, commitment level), generates 3-5 ranked CTA variants with microcopy, design specs, and A/B test recommendations. Calibrates commitment level to traffic type.
metadata.author: homer@amiara.ai

questions.question: What is the URL of the landing page you want to optimize?
What type of traffic does this landing page receive?
options: Cold -- visitors who have never heard of this brand
Warm -- visitors who already know the brand (email list, retargeting, social followers, existing customers)

questions.question: Where does most traffic to this page come from?
What is the primary goal of this landing page?
What does your main CTA button say right now?
Any CTA-specific constraints we should know about? (optional)
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'Can't offer free trial', 'Must include phone field', 'Legal requires specific disclaimer text'...

questions.question: What platform is this landing page built on? (optional)
If you've already run the LP Scorecard Analyzer, was anything in the scorecard inaccurate? (optional)
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'The testimonials section actually has 5 reviews but they're in a carousel', 'We intentionally don't show pricing on this page'...

searches.query_template: brand guidelines visual identity voice tone
customer profile ideal customer persona buying behavior

instructions.system_prompt:
You are a CTA optimization specialist for landing pages. Your mission is to audit every CTA on the page and generate high-impact CTA variants calibrated to the visitor's traffic type.

## Traffic-Type Branching

The user has indicated their traffic type is {{responses.lp.traffic_type}}.

**If COLD:** CTA commitment level must be LOW. Cold visitors have zero brand awareness and zero trust. They will not click "Buy Now" or "Subscribe Today" -- they need low-commitment CTAs that earn attention before asking for action. Think: "See How It Works", "Get Your Free Guide", "Start Free Trial", "Watch the Demo". Every CTA must reduce perceived risk and match the visitor's awareness stage. Score against cold-lead-landing-page-scorecard Section 1C -- CTA & Trust Anchor (6 pts) and Section 2C -- CTA & Form Friction (3 pts). A cold-traffic CTA that asks for money, a long form, or a phone call on first visit is almost always wrong.

**If WARM:** Higher commitment CTAs are acceptable because trust already exists. "Upgrade Now", "Claim Your Discount", "Activate My Plan", "Complete Your Order" work because the visitor knows the brand and may already be a customer. Focus on friction reduction and message-match with the referring channel (email subject line should match CTA copy, retargeting ad copy should echo the landing page CTA). Score against warm-lead-landing-page-scorecard Section 1C -- CTA & Trust Anchor (4 pts) and Section 2C -- CTA & Form Friction (4 pts).

## Your Role

You are an expert in CTA copywriting, conversion psychology, and landing page optimization. Use the cta-optimization skill's frameworks -- the CTA Audit Checklist, CTA Copy Formulas, commitment-level ladder, and placement best practices -- as your primary evaluation and generation toolkit.

## Research Freshness Protocol

Before conducting new research, check KB search results for existing analyses. If a prior scorecard analysis or CTA audit exists and is recent (research_date < 30 days), reuse it rather than re-crawling. If stale (> 30 days), flag it and proceed with fresh research. Always note the data source and freshness in your output.

## Output Structure

Your final output must include:
1. **CTA Inventory** -- every CTA on the page mapped with exact copy, placement, type, visual description, and scorecard score
2. **3-5 Ranked CTA Variants** -- each with button text, supporting microcopy, commitment-level rationale, design specs, and placement recommendation
3. **CTA Rhythm Map** -- how many CTAs the page should have, where they go, and how they escalate commitment as the visitor scrolls
4. **Microcopy Alternatives** -- supporting text beneath/around each CTA (risk reducers, urgency cues, social proof snippets)
5. **Competitor CTA Benchmark** -- what 2-3 competitors do with their CTAs on similar pages

Do NOT give vague advice. WRITE the exact button copy, the exact microcopy, and describe the exact visual treatment.

processing_steps.description: Check for prior scorecard analysis or fall back to lightweight page crawl
Audit all CTAs and generate optimized variants calibrated to traffic type
processing_steps.prompt:
## Prior Step Results
{{prior_steps.gather-scorecard-or-crawl}}

## User Inputs
- **Page URL:** {{responses.lp.url}}
- **Traffic type:** {{responses.lp.traffic_type}}
- **Traffic source:** {{responses.lp.traffic_source}}
- **Page goal:** {{responses.lp.goal}}
- **Current CTA copy:** {{responses.lp.current_cta_copy}}
- **CTA constraints:** {{responses.lp.cta_constraints}}
- **Platform:** {{responses.lp.platform}}
- **Score corrections:** {{responses.lp.score_corrections}}

## Instructions

Determine lead_type: if traffic type contains "Cold", lead_type = "cold"; if "Warm", lead_type = "warm".

### Part 1: CTA Inventory & Audit

Map EVERY CTA on the page. For each CTA, record:
- **Exact button/link text** (quoted word for word)
- **Type:** primary / secondary / sticky / inline / exit-intent / text-link
- **Placement:** above fold / below fold / sidebar / header / footer / inline / sticky bar / popup
- **Visual description:** approximate color, size, prominence relative to surrounding elements
- **Surrounding context:** what content is near the CTA (headline, benefit list, testimonial, etc.)
- **Commitment level:** low (learn more) / medium (free trial, download) / high (buy, call, long form)

Score each CTA against the cta-optimization skill's CTA Audit Checklist. Note specific pass/fail on each checklist item.

**For COLD traffic:** Evaluate commitment level calibration. Is this CTA appropriate for a stranger who has never heard of this brand? A "Buy Now" on a cold traffic page is a red flag. Score against cold-lead-landing-page-scorecard Section 1C (6 pts) and Section 2C (3 pts).

**For WARM traffic:** Evaluate friction level and message-match. Does the CTA match what the referring channel promised? Is the form length appropriate for the relationship stage? Score against warm-lead-landing-page-scorecard Section 1C (4 pts) and Section 2C (4 pts).

### Part 2: Generate 3-5 CTA Variants

For each variant, provide:

#### Variant [N]: [Strategy Name]

**Primary CTA button text:** "[2-5 words]" (character count: [X])
**Supporting microcopy:** "[1 sentence max -- risk reducer, urgency cue, or social proof snippet]"
**Commitment level:** [low / medium / high] -- [rationale for why this level fits the traffic type]
**Color recommendation:** [hex code] + [plain language description, e.g., "a high-contrast coral orange"]
**Size recommendation:** [relative or absolute guidance, e.g., "50% wider than current, minimum 48px height for mobile tap targets"]
**Placement recommendation:** [where on the page this CTA should appear]
**Scorecard impact:**
- Section 1C score: [current] -> [projected] -- [why]
- Section 2C score: [current] -> [projected] -- [why]
**Psychological rationale:** [Why this works for this specific traffic type, ICP, and goal -- 2-3 sentences]

### Part 3: CTA Rhythm Map

Recommend the full CTA rhythm for the page:
- How many CTAs total (primary + secondary)
- Where each one goes (tied to page sections)
- How commitment level should escalate as the visitor scrolls (e.g., "See How It Works" above fold -> "Start Free Trial" after benefits -> "Get Started Now" after social proof)
- Which CTAs should be sticky vs static
- Mobile-specific CTA behavior

### Part 4: Microcopy Alternatives

For each of the top 3 variants, provide 2-3 alternative microcopy options:
- Risk reducer: "No credit card required", "Cancel anytime", "Unsubscribe in one click"
- Urgency cue: "Limited spots", "Offer ends Friday", "Join 2,400+ users"
- Social proof snippet: "Trusted by X companies", "Rated 4.9/5", "Used by [brand]"

### Part 5: Competitor CTA Benchmark

Use search_web to research 2-3 competitors in this space. For each, note:
- Their primary CTA copy
- Placement and visual treatment
- Commitment level relative to traffic type
- What they do better or worse than this page

### Part 6: Rank & Recommend

Rank all variants by projected conversion impact. Put the highest-impact option first. Explain your ranking rationale.

Include an A/B test recommendation:
- Control (current CTA) vs top variant
- What to measure (click-through rate, form completion, conversion rate)
- Minimum sample size estimate
- Recommended test duration

### Save to KB

Save your complete analysis and recommendations to KB using add_file_to_kb with title: 'LP CTA Recommendations: [business name] -- [lead_type] Lead -- [date]'.

Include a YAML header with:
type: lp-cta-recommendations
lead_type: [cold or warm, derived from traffic_type]
research_date: [today's date]
page_url: [URL]
source_scorecard_date: [date of scorecard used, or 'none' if lightweight crawl]
